title: Restructure main parse loop to advance all versions before condensing
description:
ROOT CAUSE of most realworld diff failures (3 whole-file ERROR, likely 8+ localized differences).

## Problem
The Go main loop advances ONE version at a time then condenses:
```
findActiveVersion() → advanceVersion() → condenseStack() → repeat
```

The C main loop advances ALL versions before condensing:
```
for each version: advance once → then condenseStack() → repeat
```

When handleError inserts a MISSING token (e.g. MISSING "#endif"), it creates a version at state 44. recover() also creates a version at state 1 with ERROR wrapper. At EOF, recover() immediately accepts the ERROR version, setting finishedTree. Then condenseStack returns minErrorCost=MaxUint32. The main loop sees finishedTree.cost < minErrorCost and terminates — the MISSING version never gets to advance, reduce, and accept with a lower-cost tree.

## Fix
Restructure the Go main loop to match C's parser.c:2122-2176: advance all active versions (one step each) before calling condenseStack. The C loop uses `for (version = 0; version < version_count; version++)` with inner `while (is_active)` and position-based break.

## Files
- internal/parser/parser.go:263-296 (Go main loop)
- parser.c:2122-2176 (C reference)

## Regression test
testdata/regressions/c/missing_endif.input/.expected — currently fails.

## Affected files (11 failures)
Whole-file ERROR (3): curl.h, descriptor.h, Copy.pm
Localized (7): server.c, server.h, transfer.c, url.c, descriptor.cc, message.cc, Path.pm
Go generics (1): kuberuntime_manager.go (separate issue, ql0.4)
